question_text: Study leave in the public service includes the following except:
Study leave with pay and leave for in-service training should not exceed.......with a maximum........extension.
The full meaning of PRESSSID is
option_a: Preparatory Relief Enhancement Subsisdy Stabilization Strategy in Depot (PRESSSID)
option_b: Presidential Special School Scheme for Independent Development (PRESSSID)
option_c: Presidential Special Scholarship Scheme for Innovation and Development (PRESSSID)
option_d: Presidential Safe School Scheme for Innovation and Development (PRESSSID)

question_text: The conditions for study leave without pay include the following except:
option_a: the proposed course of study is contained in the approved training proposal for the MDAs of the concerned officer
option_b: Officers on study leave without pay are entitled to normal emoluments and allowances.
option_c: the duration of the study leave without pay must not be more than four years in the first instance
option_d: the period of study should not be regarded as a break in service.

question_text: An Officer may be allowed special leave from duty on full pay on compassionate ground for a period up to.........for burial of spouse/child/parents/parents of spouse.
.........is absence of an Officer from duty authorized on grounds of public policy.
Leave of absence can be in form of the following except.
option_a: Leave of absence to join spouse on grounds of public policy and to join spouse on a course of intruction of not less than nine (9) months duration abroad.
option_b: Leave of absence on public policy for Technical Aid programmes and Special / Personal Assistant on grounds of public policy.
option_c: Leave of absence for spouse of Presidents, Vice Presidents, Governors, Deputy Governors on grounds of public policy.
option_d: Leave of absence to take up political appointments such as Ministers, Commissioners, SSG, SGF.

question_text: A female officer who is nursing a child is entitled to........every day up to a maximum period of.........from the date she resumes duty from maternity leave.
A male officer is entitled to paternity leave for a period of...........and not more than once in..........for a maximum of.......... children.
The following officers are entitled to Kilometre Allowance except:
option_b: A retiring officer with spouse and four (4) children
option_c: An officer undertaking responsibility using his own car and on transfer or posting or where air transport services do not exist
option_d: An officer working with a Chief Executive, living at a very far distance to the office.

question_text: A retiring officer is entitled to.......of his gross annual emolument as...........
A newly appointed, transferred or posted officer outside his/her station is entitled to transport fare for...........as..........allowance.
option_a: Self, spouse and at most four children, Hotel accommodation allowance (1st 28 days)
option_b: Self, parents and domestic workers, Responsibility allowance
option_c: Self, Immediate supervisor and four colleagues Resettlement allowance

question_text: An officer on an approved official trip is entitled to............to pay for lodging and feeding expenses.
..............is the 30% of Duty Tour Allowance.
Officers are eligible for appropriate estacode allowance for the following reasons except:
option_b: Carrying out official duties during vacation leave abroad
option_c: Undertaking undergraduate/ postgraduate course for improving his performance in the office.
option_d: On a delegation or visit to an organization abroad for a short period.

question_text: The following officers are entitled to responsibility allowance except:
option_a: Chief Cofidential Secretaries of the President, Vice President, Senate President, SPeaker of House of Reps., CJN, SGF,HCSF, and Hon. Ministers
option_b: Assistant Chief Confidential Secretaries to Perm Sec.s. CEOs and Directors.
option_c: Principal Secretaries to the General Managers of Extra-Ministerial Offices.
option_d: Chief Confidential Secretaries to Permanent Secretaries, Chief Executives and Directors.
review_notes: Option D is missing or could not be recovered safely from the source.
Option D was editorially added because the raw source supplied only three options; correct answer remains C.

question_text: Payment for overtime on normal working days would be at the rate of.........of the monthly consolidated salary subject to a maximum of......in one month
Payment for overtime on weekends would be at the rate of................of the normal overtime rate whereas on public holidays, the rate would be............ the normal overtime rate.
review_notes: Option D was recovered from a malformed duplicate option label in the raw source: "b. 0.9 percent, Quadruple".

question_text: .......... will be paid as Resettlement allowance to a posted or transferred officer whose living condition has been disturbed by such posting or transfer
Officers on shift duties and are not Health professionals will be paid shift duty allowance at the rate of.........of their monthly salaries.
